headlines.title: Rugby star Sinfield and authors Blackman and Donaldson lead honours list
headlines.description: Noughts & Crosses author Malorie Blackman and Gruffalo creator Julia Donaldson are made dames, as the rugby league star is knighted.

headlines.title: Married at First Sight Australia stars not told partners had drug and violence convictions
headlines.description: Stars of the hit TV show say they did not know their on-screen husbands had previous convictions.

headlines.title: Deal to end fighting would lead to Hormuz reopening, Iran says
headlines.description: The deal which will pave the way for hostilities to end is close to being finalised, the US, Iran and mediators Pakistan say.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: Starmer defiant after defence spending row
headlines.description: The prime minister was keen in a BBC interview to erect the highest bar he could to any wannabe successors triggering a leadership contest.

headlines.title: Harry Styles revisits X Factor as he kicks off Wembley residency
headlines.description: The pop star reminisces about his X Factor audition, which took place near Wembley Stadium in 2010.

headlines.title: USA start tournament in style - but will they finally join the elite?
headlines.description: If there were any doubts the USA were ready to live up to the pressure of being a World Cup host, they were dispelled in a 4-1 win over Paraguay.

headlines.title: Staying up to watch the match? Here's how to survive an all-nighter
headlines.description: Forget blocks and tackles, this is the tactical plan you need for the football.

headlines.title: World Cup kicks off in the US with performances from Katy Perry, Future and Tyla
headlines.description: Earlier, Canada hosted its own opening ceremony in Toronto, featuring singers Alanis Morrisette and Michael Bublé.

headlines.title: 'It's all they're talking about': Scotland gripped by football fever
headlines.description: Excitement is building ahead of Scotland's first game in the men's World Cup for 28 years.

headlines.title: Watch: Why is Trump not at the World Cup?
headlines.description: All three leaders of the North America host nations have missed the tournament opening matches.

headlines.title: David Hockney depicted a 'peaceful, gay paradise' when homosexuality was a crime
headlines.description: The painter broke social taboos by celebrating same-sex relationships in his art.

headlines.title: Ed Sheeran found this record in a second-hand store - now it's finally getting attention
headlines.description: Allan Taylor is receiving overdue recognition after Ed Sheeran found his 1978 LP in a second-hand shop.

headlines.title: The night people in Belfast fled their homes because of racist violence
headlines.description: Reporter Richard Morgan recalls the chaotic scenes he witnessed when reporting on days of disorder in Northern Ireland.

headlines.title: Elon Musk's stratospheric rise to trillionaire status - in charts
headlines.description: The BBC breaks down how the tech mogul's fortune has grown.

headlines.title: Healey wanted UK to join global defence bank, BBC told
headlines.description: Allies of the former defence secretary claim the Treasury tried to shut down the idea.

headlines.title: England have training equipment stolen
headlines.description: England are victims of a theft of their training equipment before their arrival in Kansas City on Saturday.

headlines.title: Switzerland to vote on plan to cap population at 10 million
headlines.description: The right-wing Swiss People's Party calls the plan a "sustainability initiative",  but opponents say it is a recipe for chaos.

headlines.title: Boy, 14, among arrests after man fatally stabbed
headlines.description: Essex Police say three teenagers are being questioned on suspicion of murder.

headlines.title: Linkin Park to make history at UK's biggest rock festival
headlines.description: Linkin Park will make history as the first female-fronted band to headline Download Festival.

headlines.title: Social media ban for under-16s would be unenforceable, charity warns
headlines.description: The focus should be on banning features that make it addictive, head of the Molly Rose Foundation says.

headlines.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX soars in stock market debut
headlines.description: Musk is now worth $1.11tn according to the Bloomberg rich list, while SpaceX listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange with a value of $2.2tn.

headlines.title: Anthropic suspends new AI tools over US government security concerns
headlines.description: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, released publicly this week, sparked concerns about cybersecurity and hacking.

headlines.title: Karren Brady stayed at West Ham despite knowing of Sullivan women's team ban
headlines.description: Brady's lawyers said she had "no knowledge" of the allegations made against long-time business partner David Sullivan.

headlines.title: Shopworker sacked for tackling suspected bacon thief
headlines.description: Eileen Fox had worked at the One Stop Shop in Bootle for four years before she was sacked.

headlines.title: Palestine Action activists jailed over factory raid
headlines.description: The case is believed to be the first time that convictions for criminal damage have been classified as terrorism.

headlines.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in airstrike, Trump says
headlines.description: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

headlines.title: Ewe won't believe it: 8ft fleece sheared from sheep
headlines.description: The fleece came from a Lincoln Longwool sheep on a farm near Spilsby.

headlines.title: 'Birdwatching saved me from my gaming addiction'
headlines.description: At his lowest ebb Edward Bartlett was playing video games 20 hours a day. Now he goes birdwatching.

headlines.title: Mystery of village's elusive 'Granny H' knitter solved
headlines.description: Granny H has been leaving hundreds of knitted teddy bears, rabbits, dogs and lambs in Hartford village.

headlines.title: Painting bought for $100 in US charity shop sells for £190,000
headlines.description: Art teacher Helene Plotkin bought the work in White Plans, New York in 1966, unaware it was by Scottish Colourist FCB Cadell.

headlines.description: If there were any doubts the USA were ready to live up to the pressure of being a World Cup host, they were dispelled in a 4-1 win over Paraguay.

headlines.title: Scotland return to centre stage at last in must-win World Cup opener
headlines.description: BBC Scotland's chief sportswriter Tom English sets the scene from Boston as Scotland prepare for their first World Cup match in 28 years.

headlines.title: Club revokes Mickelson membership over allegations
headlines.description: Phil Mickelson's membership at Farms Golf Club has been revoked amid allegations of inappropriate contact with a female employee at the Californian establishment.
